title: Cohort Characterization
description: Cohort Characterization computes descriptive statistics for one or more cohorts — demographic breakdowns, comorbidity prevalence, drug utilisation, procedure frequencies, and more. Results include standardised mean differences (SMD) for comparing cohorts.
tips: Add a comparator cohort to compute SMD — values > 0.1 indicate meaningful imbalance.
Select 'All Features' to compute the full OMOP standard feature set, or pick specific domains to speed up execution.
Characterization runs in the background (Horizon queue) — you'll get a notification when done.
Export results to CSV for reporting or input into downstream tools like R's CohortMethod.
Time-windowed features (e.g., '365 days before index') are more clinically meaningful than all-time features.
